action.reasoning: Observation: New training got val 1.22 but eval previously was 0.4867 (with simpler 8-epoch training). Train loss 0.51 vs val 1.22 → strong overfitting/distribution shift. Analysis: ETTh1 val set is harder; complex model overfits. The first run (8 epochs, eval 0.4867) is what's saved as best.pt now, since current run also saved best when val=1.2229 — wait, current run replaced best.pt with val 1.22 model. Need to submit eval to see if it's better or worse. Actually the literature DLinear ETTh1 96->720 MSE is ~0.46. We got 0.4867 already. Let me try simplifying: pure NLinear (no DLinear, no per-channel) is known to be best on ETTh1 long-horizon. Decision: Submit current eval first to see new score, then maybe simplify.
